Per-feature linear + hinge basis at quantile knots, LassoCV selects sparse piecewise-linear shape per feature
LassoCV over per-feature linear+hinge basis (4 knots), then post-hoc keep top-20 coefficients for a compact formula
RidgeCV linear model plus up to 3 greedily-selected hinge corrections; mostly linear formula with a few explicit non-linear bumps
RidgeCV linear part + 3 greedy hinges; equation-style output (bakes mean-shift + hinge-at-zero into intercept so point predictions work)
LassoCV on [x, x^2] features per dimension; outputs a single-line polynomial equation in original units
LassoCV-selected top-10 features, refit via OLS; compact linear equation trying to retain high interp while improving rank
LassoCV picks feature set (cap 15), RidgeCV refit; compact linear equation, adaptive size
RidgeCV on all features to rank by |coef * std|, keep features up to 90% cumulative contribution (cap 15), refit Ridge on subset
Top-5 features via LassoCV, each with linear + quadratic term, Ridge refit; compact polynomial per-feature formula
RidgeCV on all features + single quadratic term for the top feature; RidgeCV-style output with one extra row
LassoCV-selected top-5 features, refit via OLS; ultra-compact linear equation to reduce LLM arithmetic load (rerun in clean pool)
LassoCV-selected top-8 features, refit via OLS; slightly richer than OLS5 to trade off formula length vs rank
LassoCV-selected top-12 features, refit via OLS
LassoCV-selected top-20 features, refit via OLS
LassoCV-selected top-20 features, refit with RidgeCV (mild L2 helps vs raw OLS when many features are correlated)
OLS refit on the full set of non-zero LassoCV features (no hard cap); adaptively sparse per dataset
ElasticNetCV for feature selection (handles correlated features better than pure Lasso), refit via OLS
Mutual-information feature ranking (captures non-linear dependencies Lasso misses), keep features above 5% of max MI (cap 20), OLS refit for a compact linear equation
LassoCV selects non-zero features; Huber regression refit (robust to outliers) on the selected subset; outputs the same compact linear equation as OLS-based variants
LassoCV selects non-zero features; BayesianRidge refit on the selected subset (evidence-maximized shrinkage vs RidgeCV's grid); same compact linear equation
OLS on all features, drop those whose |coef*std(x)| contribution is <2% of the top feature's, OLS refit on the retained subset
OLS on all features, drop those whose |coef*std(x)| contribution is <1% of the top feature's, OLS refit on the retained subset (gentler threshold than 2pct)
OLS on all features, drop those whose |coef*std(x)| contribution is <5% of the top feature's, OLS refit on the retained subset (stronger sparsity)
OLS on all features, drop those whose |coef*std(x)| contribution is <0.5% of the top feature's, OLS refit on the retained subset (very light pruning)
Iteratively fit OLS and prune features with |coef*std(x)| <1% of current top (up to 5 rounds); lets the threshold boundary re-settle as collinear features are removed
OLS on all features to rank by |coef*std(x)|, drop features below 1% of top contribution, RidgeCV refit on retained subset (L2 stabilizes coefs for mildly correlated features)
Forward-stepwise feature selection: greedily add features by R^2 gain, stop when gain<0.1% or 15 features, OLS refit
LassoLarsIC with BIC criterion (no CV needed) for feature selection, OLS refit on non-zero set; typically sparser than LassoCV
LassoLarsIC with AIC criterion (less aggressive than BIC, keeps more features) for feature selection, OLS refit on non-zero set
LassoLarsIC(AIC) selects features, then threshold-prune (drop |coef*std|<1% of top), OLS refit. Combines AIC's rank strength with threshold pruning's interp boost
Per-feature tercile binning: bin(x) = -1 (low), 0 (mid), +1 (high); OLS on the binned features with threshold pruning
LassoLarsIC(AIC) + OLS refit with coefficients rounded to 3 decimal places; simpler printed equation may help LLM interpretability tests
LassoLarsIC(AIC) for selection, capped at top-10 features by |coef|, OLS refit; compact formula via AIC + hard cap
LassoLarsIC(AIC) for feature selection, RidgeCV refit on selected subset for L2-stabilized coefs
OLS on all features, drop those whose |coef*std(x)| contribution is <1.5% of top, OLS refit (between 1pct and 2pct thresholds)
LARS selects top-10 features by correlation path, OLS refit on them
OLS on all features; drop features with |t-statistic| < 2; OLS refit on significant subset
Two-stage adaptive Lasso (Ridge-weighted penalty 1/|beta_ridge|) for feature selection, OLS refit on non-zero subset
Average coefficients from 30 bootstrap OLS fits (bagged OLS) for lower-variance linear model; intercept recalibrated on full data
Orthogonal Matching Pursuit selects 10 features greedily by max correlation with residual, OLS refit
LassoLarsIC(AIC) for selection, hard cap top-5 features by |coef|, OLS refit on compact subset
OLS on all features, drop those with |coef*std(x)| contribution <3% of top, OLS refit (between 2pct and 5pct)
Top-10 features by |Pearson correlation with y|, OLS refit on selected subset
OLS on all features; drop features with |t-statistic| < 1 (gentle); OLS refit
Winsorize X at 1st/99th percentile per feature, then ThresholdOLS at 1.5% contribution threshold; aims to limit outlier leverage
Stability selection: count LassoCV selection frequency across 20 bootstrap subsamples, keep features with frequency >=60%, OLS refit
LassoLarsIC(AIC) selects features, threshold-prune (drop |coef*std|<2% of top), OLS refit on final subset
LassoLarsIC(AIC) for selection, hard cap top-15 features by |coef|, OLS refit on compact subset
Huber regression (robust to y-outliers) ranks features by |coef*std|, keep those >=1.5% of top, OLS refit for final equation
